originalTitle: Burkina Faso must 'forget' about democracy, military leader says
title: Burkina Faso must 'forget' about democracy, military leader says
content: Capt Ibrahim Traoré, who seized power in 2023, says democratic rule would not work for his country.

originalTitle: Four toddlers stabbed to death at Ugandan nursery school
title: Helping Hands After Sad News in Uganda
content:
Hello, Little Friends!

Sometimes, grown-ups have to share news that makes us feel a little sad. Today, we want to talk about something sad that happened far away in a beautiful country called Uganda. It's important to remember that even when sad things happen, there are always kind people working hard to help and keep everyone safe.

What Happened?

In Uganda, there is a special place called a nursery school. A nursery school is where very young children, like toddlers, go to play, learn, and make new friends. It's a happy place where they are looked after by grown-ups. But recently, something very, very sad happened at one of these nursery schools. Some little children there were hurt, and it has made many people very sad, especially their families and friends.

Who Are the Police and What Do They Do?

When something bad or sad happens, there are special grown-ups who come to help. These grown-ups are called the police. Their job is to keep everyone safe and to find out what happened. In Uganda, the police are working very hard right now. They are trying to understand exactly what took place and to make sure something like this doesn't happen again. **Did you know?** Police officers wear special uniforms so that you can easily spot them if you need help! They are trained to protect people and help solve problems in our communities.

Nursery Schools are Special Places

Think about your school or a place where you play with other children. It's a place where you feel safe and happy, right? Nursery schools are just like that for very little children. They learn to share, to draw, to sing songs, and to play games. It’s a place where they start their big adventure of learning about the world. Teachers and other grown-ups who work at nursery schools care very much about the children. **Did you know?** Playing is a super important way for little children to learn! When they play, they are using their imagination and making their brains grow strong.

It's Okay to Feel Sad

When we hear sad news, it's very normal to feel sad. You might feel a little worried, or confused, or just a bit down. These are all normal feelings, and it's okay to talk about them with a grown-up you trust, like your mommy, daddy, a teacher, or a grandparent. Talking about your feelings can help you feel better. **Did you know?** Our feelings are like different colors! Sometimes we feel happy yellow, sometimes calm blue, and sometimes sad grey. All colors are important.

Helping Hands Come Together

Even in sad times, people show how kind and caring they can be. In Uganda, people are coming together to help the families who are very sad. This means they are offering hugs, kind words, and helping in any way they can. When something difficult happens, a community – all the people who live near each other – often becomes stronger by helping one another. **Did you know?** When many people work together, like many hands pushing a big rock, they can do amazing things that one person alone might not be able to do!

Grown-Ups Work Hard to Keep You Safe

It's very important for you to know that grown-ups – your parents, teachers, and other caregivers – work incredibly hard every single day to keep you safe. They make sure your homes are safe, your schools are safe, and the places you play are safe. They teach you about safety rules and they are always watching out for you. Things like what happened in Uganda are very, very rare. Most of the time, schools and play areas are happy, safe places for children to grow and learn. Grown-ups are always thinking about how to make things even safer.

What Happens Next?

The police in Uganda will continue their work to understand everything. The community will continue to support the families and friends who are hurting. And people everywhere will remember to be kind and caring to each other. This sad news reminds us all to cherish our friends and family and to always look out for one another. Remember, you are loved, and there are many grown-ups who care about your safety and happiness. If you ever have questions or feel worried, please talk to a grown-up you trust.
summary: Something very sad happened to some little children at a nursery school in Uganda, but police are working to understand what happened, and people are helping the families affected.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows that even when sad things happen, police and kind people in the community work together to help, keep children safe, and make sure everyone feels cared for.

originalTitle: Sixteen Kenyans missing in Russia after army recruitment
title: Our Friends are Missing: A Story from Far Away
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here. Today, we have a story that might make us feel a little bit sad, but it's important to talk about. It's about some grown-ups from our own country, Kenya.

What Happened to Our Friends?

Imagine some big brothers, sisters, or aunties deciding to go on a very long trip. They went to a country far, far away from our sunny Africa. This country is called Russia. It's a place that has lots of snow sometimes! These grown-ups went to join something called an "army." An army is like a very big team of strong people. Their job is usually to protect their own country. But these Kenyans went to join Russia's army. Now, here is the sad part. Sixteen of these wonderful Kenyan people are missing. "Missing" means that nobody knows exactly where they are right now. Their families here in Kenya are very, very worried. They want to know if their loved ones are safe.

Where is Russia? And What is an Army?

Did you know that Russia is the biggest country in the whole world? It's so big! If you wanted to fly from Kenya to Russia, it would take many, many hours on an airplane. It's really, really far away! An army is a group of soldiers. Soldiers are brave men and women who wear special uniforms. They train very hard to be strong. Most countries have their own army to keep their people safe and sound. Sometimes, armies from different countries have big disagreements. These disagreements can be very serious, and sometimes they lead to something called a "war." A war is when armies fight each other. It is a very sad and dangerous thing. Nobody wants a war. Did you know that soldiers often practice a lot to be ready? They learn how to be strong and work as a team. The country where Russia's army is fighting right now is called Ukraine. It's another country close to Russia. So, our Kenyan friends went to a faraway place where there is fighting.

Why Did They Go So Far?

This is a good question, and it's a bit tricky. Some grown-ups might choose to join an army in another country for different reasons. Maybe they wanted to learn new skills. Maybe they hoped to earn money to send back home to their families here in Kenya. Sometimes, people think it might be an exciting adventure. But going to a place where there is fighting is never truly safe. It's very important to think carefully before going to new, faraway places, especially if they are dangerous. Our Kenyan government, which is like the big leaders of our country, says that more than 250 Kenyans went to Russia. Most of them went because they wanted to. They made a choice to go.

What Does 'Missing' Mean?

When someone is "missing," it means we cannot find them. It's like when you play hide-and-seek and someone hides so well you can't find them for a long, long time. But this is not a game. This is real life and it's serious. Their families are feeling very sad and worried. They miss their sons, daughters, brothers, and sisters very much. They just want to know if they are okay and if they can come home. It's a very hard time for them, filled with many questions. Did you know that when someone is missing, people try very hard to find clues? They ask questions and look for information. It's a bit like being a detective, but for real people.

Our Government is Helping!

Don't worry, our country's leaders are not just sitting around. The people who lead Kenya, like our President and ministers, are working hard. They are talking to the people who lead Russia. This means they are sending messages and having important calls. They are asking questions. They want to know what happened to the sixteen missing Kenyans. They want to make sure everyone is safe. Our government cares very much about all Kenyans, no matter where they are in the world. Did you know that our Kenyan government works hard every day to keep all of us safe right here at home? They also try to help Kenyans who are in other countries. They are like big guardians for our whole country.

Let's Talk About Staying Safe

This story teaches us something very important, kids. It teaches us about staying safe. Always remember to talk to your parents or guardians before you go anywhere new or far away. It's very important to tell them your plans. It's very important to listen to the grown-ups who take care of you. They know what is best and what places might be dangerous. Never go with strangers, and always tell someone you trust where you are going. Your safety is the most important thing! Going to a different country, especially one where there might be problems like fighting, is a very big decision. It's best to stay close to home and your family, where you are safe and loved. Always ask a grown-up if you are unsure.

Sending Hope and Prayers

Even though this is a sad story, we can all send our good wishes and prayers. Let's hope that the sixteen missing Kenyans are found soon. Let's hope they are safe and can come back home to their families who love them so much. Our big African family cares about everyone. We wish for peace everywhere in the world, so no one has to go to dangerous places. We hope that all Kenyans, wherever they are, are safe and sound. We will keep you updated if we hear more news. Stay safe, little superstars!
summary: Sixteen Kenyans who traveled to Russia to join its army are now missing, causing worry among their families and prompting the Kenyan government to seek answers about their safety.
whyMatters: This matters because grown-ups from Kenya went to a faraway country where there is fighting, and now some of them are missing. It shows us how important it is to stay safe and listen to our families, and how our government tries to help Kenyans everywhere.

originalTitle: War crimes are no longer shameful. That should terrify you
title: War crimes are no longer shameful. That should terrify you
content: In the latest escalation in the Middle East, warring sides have demonstrated complete disregard for civilian life.

originalTitle: ‘I don’t know how we’ll emerge from this’: How much more can Israelis take?
title: Being Brave and Helping Others in a Special Land!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! Your kid-friendly reporter is here with a story about a special place far away. Imagine a land called Israel, filled with sunshine, ancient stories, and very, very brave people. Sometimes, in life, and in different parts of the world, people face big, big challenges. These challenges can be like a long, tricky maze that everyone has to walk through together. For a long, long time, the people in Israel have been walking through such a maze. Grown-ups who study countries tell us that when times are tough for so long, many things in a country can change. It can change how grown-ups make rules, how families get their food and toys, and even how everyone lives together as friends and neighbors.

What are These Tricky Times Like?

Imagine living in a place where sometimes it's hard to feel completely safe, or where grown-ups worry a lot about tomorrow. That's a little bit like the tricky times we are talking about. It's not about playing games, but about real life challenges that make everyone work extra hard and stick together. These challenges are not like a quick thunderstorm; they have been going on for a long, long time. When something lasts for so long, it starts to change many, many things around it, just like a strong river can change the shape of the land over time. Our story today is about how people in Israel, young and old, have learned to be super strong and clever, and how they help each other through these long, tricky times. It's about being a community and finding ways to smile, even when things are tough.

How Grown-Ups Make Rules (It's Called 'Politics'!)

In every country, grown-ups have special jobs where they help make rules for everyone. This is like when your parents make rules at home, or your teacher makes rules in the classroom. When times are tough in a country like Israel, these grown-ups have to work even harder to make sure everyone is safe and has what they need. Sometimes, when things are very difficult, more people might want to help make these important decisions. It's like when your whole family sits together to decide something big, like what to do on holiday. Everyone wants to help make the best choice so that life can be good for everyone. They try to find new ways to help families, make schools safe, and make sure hospitals have what they need. It’s a very big job, and it needs lots of teamwork and kindness from all the grown-ups who are making the rules. Did you know? In many countries, people vote to choose who makes the rules. It's like choosing the captain of your school team, but for a whole country!

How People Get Food and Toys (It's Called 'Economy'!)

Every day, people need food to eat, clothes to wear, and sometimes, even toys to play with! Grown-ups go to work to earn money so they can buy these things. This is how the 'economy' works. When a country is going through very long, tricky times, it can be harder for grown-ups to do their jobs, or for shops to have all the things people need. Imagine if it was harder for farmers to grow food, or for trucks to bring food to the shops. That would be a big problem! So, people in Israel have learned to be very clever. They might find new ways to share things, or new ways to make things in their own country so they don't have to buy everything from far away. Families might learn to grow some of their own vegetables, or make their own clothes. Everyone tries to save and be smart with what they have. It's all about helping each other make sure no one goes without the important things. They show us how strong and creative people can be when they work together. Did you know? Before money was invented, people used to trade things! A farmer might trade corn for a shoemaker's shoes.

How Everyone Lives Together (It's Called 'Society'!)

Our 'society' is all of us – our families, our friends, our neighbors, and everyone in our town or village. It's how we live, play, and learn together. When things are difficult for a long time, it can change how people feel and act towards each other. But often, it makes people even closer! In Israel, people have learned to stick together like super glue. They help their neighbors, they look out for each other's children, and they make sure no one feels alone. When times are tough, it's really important to have good friends and family to laugh with, to talk to, and to help you feel brave. Children still go to school and play games, and grown-ups try their best to keep everything as normal as possible. They often have special groups that help people who are feeling sad or scared, reminding everyone that they are not alone and that help is always there. Did you know? Humans are very social creatures! We love to be with our friends and family, and helping others makes us feel happy inside.

Children Are Superheroes!

Even though grown-ups worry a lot during tricky times, children in Israel are amazing little superheroes! They are strong, brave, and full of hope. They still love to play their games, draw pictures, and learn at school. Grown-ups try their very best to keep schools open and playgrounds safe so that kids can still be kids. It’s so important for children to learn and play, no matter what. It helps them feel happy and strong inside. These children learn very quickly how to be kind, how to help, and how to find joy in small things, like a sunny day or a new friend. They show everyone that even when big challenges are happening, there is still so much good in the world, and so much hope for the future. They are like little lights, shining brightly and reminding everyone to keep going. Did you know? Playing helps children learn important things like sharing, solving problems, and being creative!

Hope for a Brighter Tomorrow

Even when the maze is long and tricky, people in Israel always hold onto hope. They believe that tomorrow can be better. They work very hard every day, helping each other, being kind, and never giving up. Just like the sun always rises after the darkest night, people believe that good times will come again. And because they are so strong and help each other so much, they make their country a truly special place filled with brave hearts. So, my friends, let's all send a big cheer to the brave people of Israel! Let's remember to be kind to our neighbors, help our friends, and always, always keep hope in our hearts, no matter what big challenges come our way. You are all amazing, and together, we can make the world a kinder place!
summary: People in Israel have faced many challenges for a long time, changing how grown-ups make rules, how families get food, and how everyone lives together, but they are strong and always help each other.
whyMatters: This story helps us understand that people in some countries face very big challenges, and it shows us how important it is for everyone to be brave, kind, and help each other, no matter where they live.

originalTitle: Burkina Faso military leader Traore says ‘forget democracy’
title: Burkina Faso military leader Traore says ‘forget democracy’
content: Traore reneged on promises to hold elections and has since scrapped political parties in the country.

originalTitle: Artemis II leaves Earth's orbit on track for far side of the Moon
title: Artemis II leaves Earth's orbit on track for far side of the Moon
content: The mission's last, big push on its lunar journey takes humans out of the Earth's orbit for the first time since 1972.

originalTitle: Watch: Artemis II astronauts speak from space
title: Watch: Artemis II astronauts speak from space
content: The crew on board the Artemis II spoke by video link, sharing messages for their families and experiences of the trip so far.

originalTitle: Trump removes US Attorney General Pam Bondi
title: Trump removes US Attorney General Pam Bondi
content: Bondi's time as top US law enforcement officer was overshadowed by her handling of the Epstein files.

originalTitle: Chelsea's Fernandez dropped after 'crossing a line'
title: Chelsea's Fernandez dropped after 'crossing a line'
content: Chelsea's Enzo Fernandez will sit out their next two games after he "crossed a line", says manager Liam Rosenior.

originalTitle: Isak could make injury return against Man City
title: Isak could make injury return against Man City
content: Alexander Isak could feature in Liverpool's FA Cup quarter-final against Manchester City after missing more than three months through injury.

title: Italy and Gattuso 'mutually terminate' contract
content: Manager Gennaro Gattuso and Italy agree to "mutually terminate" his contract after their failure to qualify for a third successive World Cup.
